page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system provides 48 virtual CPUs (24 physical cores with 2 threads per core) and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory clocked at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 1800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core execution scales effectively, achieving average marks in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Notably, the processor demonstrates strong cryptographic capabilities, placing in the top 25% for Passmark CPU encryption. Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, consistently perform in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5ad.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores with high-speed local NVMe storage. This hardware profile is well-suited for workloads that require a balance of compute and memory alongside fast local scratch space. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded applications, web serving, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications that can leverage the local NVMe SSDs for caching or temporary storage, while avoiding tasks heavily dependent on single-core performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 10 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between poor single-core execution and average multi-core capabilities, with strong cryptographic performance in the top 25%. The instance delivers average memory bandwidth and database throughput. Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and fast local instance storage, the m5ad.12xlarge is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and web serving applications that benefit from local NVMe scratch space but do not require high single-threaded performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.

Includes 1800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
